name: repo-completeness-panel
title: Full Repo Completeness Audit
description: Coordinated dispatch of all 8 repo-completeness agents with synthesis. Use for whole-repo audits; produces unified gap_ledger + repo_review_report.

phases.purpose: Build repo map first; downstream agents consume it
Specialist audits run in parallel; each consumes the repo map
Merge findings, dedupe, rank, sequence

use_when: User wants complete whole-repo audit with all dimensions covered.

confirmation.reason: Read-only or proposal-only panel. Confirm before applying any generated remediation or runtime action.

success_criteria: critical repo surfaces are mapped before audit
findings are deduplicated with evidence and severity
gaps are sequenced into immediate, near-term, and later work

map.input: repo-completeness-panel scope plus outputs from prior phases
map.output: map phase artifact with evidence, assumptions, and open questions
map.quality_gate: map output is specific enough for the next phase to consume without re-discovery

audit.input: repo-completeness-panel scope plus outputs from prior phases
audit.output: audit phase artifact with evidence, assumptions, and open questions
audit.quality_gate: audit output is specific enough for the next phase to consume without re-discovery

synthesize.input: repo-completeness-panel scope plus outputs from prior phases
synthesize.output: synthesize phase artifact with evidence, assumptions, and open questions
synthesize.quality_gate: synthesize output is specific enough for the next phase to consume without re-discovery

do_not_use_when: Use feature-gap-panel for one feature, release-gate-panel for ship/no-ship, or codebase-health for lighter hygiene.

memory_policy.notes: May propose memory candidates from repeatable findings; promotion remains explicit.

learning_policy.notes: May queue learning candidates for routing or workflow lessons; application remains review-gated.

dream_policy.notes: May use existing dream reports as context; does not launch background analysis without user approval.

name: feature-gap-panel
title: Single-Feature End-to-End Audit
description: Focused panel for ONE named feature. Cartographer + feature-completeness + wiring + test-gap, synthesized.

phases.purpose: Map the feature surface (scoped to user's argument)
Three lenses on one feature
Single prioritized gap list with fix sequence

use_when: User names ONE feature and wants end-to-end completeness verified.

confirmation.reason: Read-only or proposal-only panel. Confirm before applying any generated remediation or runtime action.

inputs: relevant routes, commands, or user flows
success_criteria: feature entry points and persistence boundaries are mapped
completeness, wiring, and test risks are separated
fix sequence is narrow enough for one implementation slice

map.input: feature-gap-panel scope plus outputs from prior phases
map.output: map phase artifact with evidence, assumptions, and open questions
map.quality_gate: map output is specific enough for the next phase to consume without re-discovery

audit.input: feature-gap-panel scope plus outputs from prior phases
audit.output: audit phase artifact with evidence, assumptions, and open questions
audit.quality_gate: audit output is specific enough for the next phase to consume without re-discovery

synthesize.input: feature-gap-panel scope plus outputs from prior phases
synthesize.output: synthesize phase artifact with evidence, assumptions, and open questions
synthesize.quality_gate: synthesize output is specific enough for the next phase to consume without re-discovery

do_not_use_when: Do not use for whole-repo audits, speculative product ideation, or release decisions without a named feature.

memory_policy.notes: May propose memory candidates from repeatable findings; promotion remains explicit.

learning_policy.notes: May queue learning candidates for routing or workflow lessons; application remains review-gated.

dream_policy.notes: May use existing dream reports as context; does not launch background analysis without user approval.

name: contract-drift-panel
title: API/Schema/Event Contract Drift Audit
description: Producer-consumer contract review across all integration boundaries.

phases.purpose: Identify contract surfaces (APIs, schemas, events)
Find drift; identify which contracts lack tests
Prioritized contract gap list with migration sequencing

confirmation.reason: Read-only or proposal-only panel. Confirm before applying any generated remediation or runtime action.

success_criteria: producer-consumer pairs are identified
drift is classified as breaking, risky, or benign
missing contract tests are named

title: Pre-Release Ship/No-Ship Audit
description: Release readiness with security + test coverage + adversarial review.

Four lenses on shipability
Final ship/no-ship verdict with phased remediation

blockers are backed by evidence

name: prd-panel
title: Full PRD with cross-disciplinary review
description: Coordinated PRD drafting + technical design + measurement + risk review.

phases.purpose: Problem, users, goals, non-goals, requirements, scope
Technical design + measurement strategy in parallel
Compliance, privacy, operational risk assessment
Merge all outputs into final PRD with risk-acknowledged scope

use_when: User wants a PRD that has gone through technical, measurement, and risk review before engineering kickoff.

confirmation.reason: Read-only or proposal-only panel. Confirm before applying any generated remediation or runtime action.

success_criteria: problem, goals, non-goals, and requirements are explicit
technical feasibility and measurement strategy are reviewed
risk controls are integrated into scope

product.input: prd-panel scope plus outputs from prior phases
product.output: product phase artifact with evidence, assumptions, and open questions
product.quality_gate: product output is specific enough for the next phase to consume without re-discovery

design_and_measure.input: prd-panel scope plus outputs from prior phases
design_and_measure.output: design_and_measure phase artifact with evidence, assumptions, and open questions
design_and_measure.quality_gate: design_and_measure output is specific enough for the next phase to consume without re-discovery

name: ai-feature-panel
title: AI/LLM Feature Spec with safety review
description: AI-specific PRD with technical design, eval methodology, security review, and risk-and-controls review. The 4-agent chain for any LLM/agent/ML feature.

phases.purpose: Problem framing, users, goals, non-goals (AI-aware)
Technical design with AI-specific sections + eval methodology
Security review for AI surface + compliance/privacy/data-handling
Final AI feature spec with safety controls, fallbacks, cost model

use_when: User is building an LLM/agent/ML feature and wants the full AI-product review chain before implementation.

confirmation.reason: Read-only or proposal-only panel. Confirm before applying any generated remediation or runtime action.

success_criteria: AI behavior is scoped with non-goals and fallbacks
evaluation plan covers quality and safety
security and privacy controls are concrete

product.input: ai-feature-panel scope plus outputs from prior phases

name: idea-panel
title: Coordinated Idea Generation + Validation
description: 4-agent panel for early-stage ideation with customer + market grounding. Used pre-PRD when exploring a new problem space.

phases.purpose: Frame the problem with multiple lenses; identify generation method
Customer signal interpretation + competitive landscape in parallel
Generate ideas grounded in customer + market context; triage with weighted criteria
Convert top recommendations into concept briefs ready for stakeholder review

use_when: User is pre-PRD, exploring a problem space, and wants ideas grounded in customer and market reality with structured triage.

confirmation.reason: Read-only or proposal-only panel. Confirm before applying any generated remediation or runtime action.

success_criteria: ideas are grounded in user and market evidence
trade-offs and disconfirming signals are visible
top options are ranked by explicit criteria

frame.input: idea-panel scope plus outputs from prior phases
frame.output: frame phase artifact with evidence, assumptions, and open questions
frame.quality_gate: frame output is specific enough for the next phase to consume without re-discovery

ground.input: idea-panel scope plus outputs from prior phases
ground.output: ground phase artifact with evidence, assumptions, and open questions
ground.quality_gate: ground output is specific enough for the next phase to consume without re-discovery

validate_recommendation.quality_gate: validate_recommendation output is specific enough for the next phase to consume without re-discovery

do_not_use_when: Do not use once scope is chosen and the user needs MVP definition, PRD, or implementation.

name: mvp-panel
title: MVP Scope Definition with Evaluation Strategy
description: 3-agent panel for scoping the MVP with hypothesis, validation strategy, and technical feasibility check. Used after concept-stage, pre-engineering kickoff.

phases.purpose: Define MVP hypothesis, must/should/won't-have, validation criteria
Technical feasibility check + validation strategy design in parallel
Final MVP scope with technical confidence + validation plan

use_when: User has a chosen concept and needs MVP scope with both technical feasibility and validation strategy.

confirmation.reason: Read-only or proposal-only panel. Confirm before applying any generated remediation or runtime action.

success_criteria: MVP hypothesis and must-have behavior are explicit
should-have and won't-have items prevent scope creep
validation plan can be run after implementation

scope.input: mvp-panel scope plus outputs from prior phases
scope.output: scope phase artifact with evidence, assumptions, and open questions
scope.quality_gate: scope output is specific enough for the next phase to consume without re-discovery

feasibility_and_evaluation.input: mvp-panel scope plus outputs from prior phases
feasibility_and_evaluation.output: feasibility_and_evaluation phase artifact with evidence, assumptions, and open questions
feasibility_and_evaluation.quality_gate: feasibility_and_evaluation output is specific enough for the next phase to consume without re-discovery

synthesize.quality_gate: synthesize output is specific enough for the next phase to consume without re-discovery

do_not_use_when: Do not use for broad ideation, full PRD drafting, or implementation once MVP scope is already locked.

memory_policy.notes: May read relevant repository memory; does not write or promote memory entries.

learning_policy.notes: Does not create or apply learning candidates by default.

dream_policy.notes: May consume existing dream reports when relevant; does not start background dream jobs.

name: cognitive-governance-panel
title: Cognitive Governance and Learning Health Review
description: V8 governance panel for memory, learning queue, dream reports, pathfinder routing, dashboard telemetry, and capability graph health.

confirmation.reason: Reviews and proposes governance actions. Memory promotion, learning application, and dream execution stay explicit.

inputs: plugin root or installed runtime path
success_criteria: cognitive surfaces are checked together
state writes and approval boundaries are explicit
telemetry gaps are separated from usage gaps

inventory.input: cognitive-governance-panel scope plus outputs from prior phases
inventory.output: inventory phase artifact with evidence, assumptions, and open questions
inventory.quality_gate: inventory output is specific enough for the next phase to consume without re-discovery

governance_audit.input: cognitive-governance-panel scope plus outputs from prior phases
governance_audit.output: governance_audit phase artifact with evidence, assumptions, and open questions
governance_audit.quality_gate: governance_audit output is specific enough for the next phase to consume without re-discovery

route_and_workflow.input: cognitive-governance-panel scope plus outputs from prior phases
route_and_workflow.output: route_and_workflow phase artifact with evidence, assumptions, and open questions
route_and_workflow.quality_gate: route_and_workflow output is specific enough for the next phase to consume without re-discovery

synthesize.input: cognitive-governance-panel scope plus outputs from prior phases
synthesize.output: synthesize phase artifact with evidence, assumptions, and open questions
synthesize.quality_gate: synthesize output is specific enough for the next phase to consume without re-discovery

do_not_use_when: Do not use for ordinary feature work, code review, or runtime bug fixes that do not touch V8 cognitive surfaces.

phases.purpose: Map cognitive surfaces, catalog links, dashboard telemetry, and graph contracts
Audit memory, learning queue, and dream report health in parallel
Check pathfinder tags, routing affordances, and safe workflow recommendations
Merge findings into governance recommendations and validation gates

use_when: V8 governance panel for memory, learning queue, dream reports, pathfinder routing, dashboard telemetry, and capability graph health.

memory_policy.notes: Audits memory quality and proposes candidates; never promotes memory automatically.

learning_policy.notes: Audits and queues learning candidates; application is explicitly review-gated.

dream_policy.notes: Reviews existing dream reports and recommends jobs; running jobs remains explicit.

name: security-privacy-panel
title: Security and Privacy Control Review
description: Cross-functional security, privacy, data-flow, and control review for sensitive code, AI surfaces, integrations, or release candidates.

confirmation.reason: Read-only audit. Remediation, credential rotation, or policy changes require explicit approval.

success_criteria: data flows and trust boundaries are mapped
findings are ranked by exploitability and impact
controls are tied to concrete risks

map.input: security-privacy-panel scope plus outputs from prior phases
map.output: map phase artifact with evidence, assumptions, and open questions
map.quality_gate: map output is specific enough for the next phase to consume without re-discovery

audit.input: security-privacy-panel scope plus outputs from prior phases

synthesize.input: security-privacy-panel scope plus outputs from prior phases
synthesize.output: synthesize phase artifact with evidence, assumptions, and open questions
synthesize.quality_gate: synthesize output is specific enough for the next phase to consume without re-discovery

do_not_use_when: Do not use for generic dependency audits without security/privacy context or for code review unrelated to sensitive boundaries.

phases.purpose: Map sensitive code paths, data flows, and trust boundaries
Security, privacy, and operational controls review in parallel
Translate findings into architecture and control recommendations
Produce ranked report with remediation, owners, and validation gates

use_when: Cross-functional security, privacy, data-flow, and control review for sensitive code, AI surfaces, integrations, or release candidates.

memory_policy.notes: May propose memory candidates from repeatable findings; promotion remains explicit.

learning_policy.notes: May queue learning candidates for routing or workflow lessons; application remains review-gated.

dream_policy.notes: May use existing dream reports as context; does not launch background analysis without user approval.

name: migration-readiness-panel
title: Migration Readiness and Compatibility Review
description: Migration planning panel for version upgrades, API swaps, schema migrations, runtime changes, or package moves.

confirmation.reason: Produces readiness and sequencing guidance. Applying migration changes requires explicit implementation approval.

success_criteria: current and target contract surfaces are mapped
breaking changes and data risks are explicit
test and rollout gates are named

map.input: migration-readiness-panel scope plus outputs from prior phases
map.output: map phase artifact with evidence, assumptions, and open questions
map.quality_gate: map output is specific enough for the next phase to consume without re-discovery

audit.input: migration-readiness-panel scope plus outputs from prior phases

synthesize.output: synthesize phase artifact with evidence, assumptions, and open questions
synthesize.quality_gate: synthesize output is specific enough for the next phase to consume without re-discovery

do_not_use_when: Do not use for trivial dependency bumps, exploratory architecture reviews, or one-file refactors without migration risk.

phases.purpose: Map current state, target state, and affected integration surfaces
Compatibility, test, and release readiness review in parallel
Split migration into safe phases with rollback and validation
Merge findings into readiness verdict and migration execution plan

use_when: Migration planning panel for version upgrades, API swaps, schema migrations, runtime changes, or package moves.

memory_policy.notes: May propose memory candidates from repeatable findings; promotion remains explicit.

learning_policy.notes: May queue learning candidates for routing or workflow lessons; application remains review-gated.

name: incident-response-panel
title: Incident Response and Recovery Review
description: Fast triage panel for production incidents, broken releases, live regressions, data exposure concerns, or urgent runtime failures.

confirmation.reason: Can recommend high-impact recovery, rollback, or credential actions. Execution always requires explicit confirmation.

success_criteria: likely causes are ranked by evidence
impact and containment are separated
recovery actions are sequenced by risk

triage.input: incident-response-panel scope plus outputs from prior phases
triage.output: triage phase artifact with evidence, assumptions, and open questions
triage.quality_gate: triage output is specific enough for the next phase to consume without re-discovery

impact.input: incident-response-panel scope plus outputs from prior phases
impact.output: impact phase artifact with evidence, assumptions, and open questions
impact.quality_gate: impact output is specific enough for the next phase to consume without re-discovery

recovery_plan.input: incident-response-panel scope plus outputs from prior phases
recovery_plan.output: recovery_plan phase artifact with evidence, assumptions, and open questions
recovery_plan.quality_gate: recovery_plan output is specific enough for the next phase to consume without re-discovery

writeup.input: incident-response-panel scope plus outputs from prior phases
writeup.output: writeup phase artifact with evidence, assumptions, and open questions
writeup.quality_gate: writeup output is specific enough for the next phase to consume without re-discovery

do_not_use_when: Do not use for routine debugging with no operational impact, speculative architecture work, or product planning.

phases.purpose: Reproduce or bound the failure and rank likely causes by evidence
Assess security, operational, and release impact in parallel
Prioritize containment, recovery, validation, and follow-up work
Produce concise incident report and postmortem scaffold

use_when: Fast triage panel for production incidents, broken releases, live regressions, data exposure concerns, or urgent runtime failures.

memory_policy.notes: May propose memory candidates from repeatable findings; promotion remains explicit.

learning_policy.notes: May queue learning candidates for routing or workflow lessons; application remains review-gated.

dream_policy.notes: May use existing dream reports as context; does not launch background analysis without user approval.

name: experience-quality-panel
title: Experience Quality Panel
description: Cross-functional product UI quality panel for important frontend, design-system, visual QA, accessibility-adjacent, or interaction-polish work. Combines repo mapping, design critique, accessibility/runtime audit, test strategy, and synthesis into a concrete experience-quality report.

phases.purpose: Map the relevant frontend entrypoints, design-system sources, routes, run commands, and validation tools before critique
Review visual/product quality and accessibility-adjacent risks independently with evidence
Turn the critique into screenshot, responsive, state, and regression validation coverage
Deduplicate findings, preserve strengths, rank fixes, and produce an implementation-ready experience-quality report

use_when: Use for high-stakes frontend quality, visual polish, product taste, responsive QA, or design-system work where one reviewer is likely to miss cross-cutting issues.

confirmation.reason: Read-only critique and validation planning panel. Confirm before applying any generated UI changes.

inputs: surface route, screenshot, component, or design-system path
success_criteria: actual frontend or design-system surface is mapped before critique
visual, interaction, a11y-adjacent, and validation findings cite concrete evidence
recommendations are ranked by user impact and implementation leverage
follow-up validation names exact screenshots, states, commands, or tests

surface_map.input: experience-quality-panel scope plus repository path and target surface
surface_map.output: surface map with entrypoints, routes, design-system sources, run commands, and unknowns
surface_map.quality_gate: downstream agents can inspect the right rendered or source surface without rediscovery

critique.input: surface map and available screenshots/routes/source files
critique.output: independent design and a11y-adjacent findings with evidence and severity
critique.quality_gate: each finding cites a concrete route, screenshot, component, token, or state

validation_strategy.output: risk-weighted visual/regression validation plan with viewports, states, and commands
validation_strategy.quality_gate: the plan can verify each high/medium finding after implementation

synthesize.input: surface map, critique findings, and validation strategy
synthesize.output: deduplicated experience-quality report with ranked fixes and preserved strengths
synthesize.quality_gate: report is implementation-ready and distinguishes must-fix defects from subjective preferences

do_not_use_when: Use design-review for lightweight critique, frontend-visual-qa for a single surface with immediate implementation, accessibility-review for WCAG-only audits, or review for code diff correctness.

memory_policy.notes: May propose reusable design-system or product-quality preferences as memory candidates; promotion remains explicit.

learning_policy.notes: May queue route-learning candidates for visual QA/design phrasing; application remains review-gated.

dream_policy.notes: May use existing dream reports as context; does not launch background design analysis without user approval.
